skill.name: square-automation
skill.description: Automate Square tasks via Rube MCP (Composio): payments, orders, invoices, locations. Always search tools first for current schemas.
skill.summary: Automate Square payment processing, order management, and invoicing through Composio's Square toolkit via Rube MCP.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. The detected 'external_commands' patterns (57 instances) are Ruby/shell backtick syntax examples in markdown documentation, not executable code. The network detection is a documentation URL (https://rube.app/mcp) showing where to obtain the Rube MCP server. This skill is legitimate documentation for automating Square payment tasks with no executable code or security risks.

content.value_statement: Streamline Square payment processing and order management without manual API calls. Automate invoicing, payment tracking, and location management through Claude Code.
actual_capabilities: List and monitor Square payments with date range filtering
Search and retrieve orders by location, status, and date
Manage Square business locations and retrieve location details
Create, view, and cancel Square invoices
Cancel pending payments and update order states
Handle pagination for large result sets
limitations: Requires active Square OAuth connection via Rube MCP server
Cannot create new payments or process transactions directly
Read-only access to payment history (cannot modify completed payments)
Rate limits apply for bulk operations
Location IDs must be resolved before most operations

use_cases.title: Payment Reconciliation
use_cases.description: Business owners can automatically list and analyze daily payments across all Square locations to reconcile accounts and identify discrepancies.

use_cases.title: Order Status Tracking
use_cases.description: Support teams can search and retrieve order details by customer name or date to provide real-time order status updates without accessing Square dashboard.

use_cases.title: Invoice Workflow Automation
use_cases.description: Finance teams can list scheduled and unpaid invoices, cancel invalid invoices, and export invoice data for accounting systems.

prompt_templates.title: List Recent Payments
prompt_templates.prompt: List all Square payments from the last 7 days. Show payment IDs, amounts, and statuses.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick payment overview for cash flow monitoring

prompt_templates.title: Find Orders by Date
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for all completed orders at 'Main Street' location between January 1st and January 31st. Show order IDs and total amounts.

prompt_templates.title: Cancel Pending Invoice
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the unpaid invoice for customer 'John Doe' sent this month and cancel it using the correct version number.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Location Payment Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Retrieve payments from all three locations for Q1 2024. Group by location and calculate totals. Handle pagination automatically.

output_examples.output:

Total: $444.50

- Order #ORD-001: $350.00 (COMPLETED)
- Order #ORD-002: $125.00 (OPEN)
- Order #ORD-003: $75.00 (COMPLETED)

1. Downtown Store (L1234ABCD) - ACTIVE
2. Uptown Branch (L5678EFGH) - ACTIVE
3. Warehouse (L9012IJKL) - INACTIVE

best_practices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to get current tool schemas before executing workflows
Cache location IDs after initial retrieval to avoid redundant API calls
Use RFC 3339 timestamp format for all date range filters (e.g., 2024-01-01T00:00:00Z)
Handle pagination by following cursor values until absent for large result sets
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to cancel completed payments - use refund workflows instead
Avoid calling UPDATE_ORDER without first retrieving the current version number
Never hardcode location IDs - always resolve them dynamically via LIST_LOCATIONS
Do not skip pagination handling - large datasets will return incomplete results

faq.question: Do I need a Square API key?
faq.answer: No. This skill uses Rube MCP which handles Square OAuth authentication. Just add https://rube.app/mcp as your MCP server and complete the OAuth flow.

faq.question: Can I process new payments through this skill?
faq.answer: No. This skill can list, cancel, and monitor existing payments, but cannot create new payment transactions. Use Square's checkout flows for new payments.

faq.question: Why do I get 'version mismatch' errors when updating orders?
faq.answer: UPDATE_ORDER requires the current version field. Always retrieve the order first with SQUARE_RETRIEVE_ORDER to get the latest version number before updating.

faq.question: How do I find my location IDs?
faq.answer: Call SQUARE_LIST_LOCATIONS to retrieve all locations. Each location has an 'id' field (e.g., 'L1234ABCD') that you use for filtering orders and payments.

faq.question: Can I cancel completed payments?
faq.answer: No. Only pending payments can be cancelled. For completed payments that need reversal, use Square's refund process instead.

faq.question: What happens when I have more than 100 results?
faq.answer: Square paginates results. Check the response 'cursor' field and pass it to the next request. Continue until the cursor is absent to get all results.
